text: Direction Fabric must not add more lifecycle features until agents can discover and use the protocol through root AGENTS.md, current-thread defaults, fabric status, and low-friction sync/note commands.

Fabric must make direction propagation work across separate agent threads and worktrees; if repo-local git-backed storage is the sync mechanism, the tracked/ignored .fabric policy and agent protocol must not silently isolate direction per worktree.

Fabric now uses a git-common shared event mirror so sibling worktrees can see new direction before the durable .fabric/ledger/events.jsonl changes are committed; local current-thread and generated checkpoint files remain per-worktree state.
Before adding more lifecycle features, Fabric must harden shared ledger safety: concurrent writes, event ID uniqueness, promotion safety, and ledger health checks.
Shared ledger state should support multiple agent worktrees without requiring every live direction to be committed to Git.
V3 is manual PR/review ingestion. It should create candidate review directions from explicit review feedback and feed continue/handoff context before adding GitHub App/webhooks.
Do not ingest every review comment as direction.
reason: The ledger should stay useful and sparse.
rejected_paths: Treating all review comments as candidate project direction
Replaying raw review threads into continuation context
preferred_paths: Ingest only feedback that changes what another agent should do next
Keep nits/checklist items live or ignored

evidence.text: simulated review feedback during V3 dogfood

text: V4 closes the Fabric lifecycle by reviewing candidate/live/review/challenge direction after PR or issue completion and promoting only useful lessons into durable project direction.
